page: Microsoft Azure D16alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. This server features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 945 GB local disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing benchmarks both rank in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core scores fall within the top 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, align with the middle 50% average. Uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% tier at 36.92 GB/sec, whereas small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth remain average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D16alds_v6 offers a compute-heavy profile with a lower memory-to-CPU ratio, making it a resource-dense option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the limited 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and the weaker uncached memory read speeds, which may constrain memory-intensive applications. This server is suited for workloads such as single-threaded processing, text processing, web serving, lightweight database operations, and software compilation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It includes a 945 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, with no GPU acceleration. This instance exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-core Geekbench scores. However, its multi-core performance and memory write speeds sit in the average tier, while uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, landing in the bottom 25%. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this server represents a compute-dense option for workloads that prioritize raw single-thread processing over high memory capacity. It is suited for text processing, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16alds_v6 is a 16-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 32 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, offering top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capacity.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16alds_v6 features 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual server featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance, local storage, and a lean memory-to-CPU ratio.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
945 GB local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Weak uncached large-block read memory bandwidth of 36.92 GB/sec
Best suited for text processing, software compilation, and web serving
